project_type: C++20 desktop cybersecurity platform with static libraries
build_system: CMake 3.22 with static library modules. Each src/<module>/ has CMakeLists.txt defining a static lib, include/ for public headers, src/ for implementation.
test_framework: doctest (header-only). All tests in tests/ directory organized by module. Single test_main.cpp with DOCTEST_CONFIG_IMPLEMENT_WITH_MAIN. Tests registered in tests/CMakeLists.txt.
verification_instructions: 1. Run cmake -B build -DENABLE_QT=OFF. 2. Run cmake --build build. 3. cd build && ctest --output-on-failure. All 824+ existing tests must pass plus new tests.
setup_instructions: Build already works. cmake -B build -DENABLE_QT=OFF && cmake --build build
environment_constraints: INTEGRATIONS_ONLY network - no external downloads. CMake 3.22, GCC 11.5, Clang 15 pre-installed. Third-party libs vendored in third_party/ (spdlog, nlohmann_json, doctest, sqlite3 stub). Do NOT push - just commit locally.
contribution_requirements: Follow existing patterns: interface header I*.h, implementation header *.h, implementation *.cpp. Use ThreatOne::Core::ModuleLogger for logging. Use Core::Result<T> for error handling. Thread-safe with std::mutex. Use [[nodiscard]] on getters.
key_patterns: Each module follows: IFoo.h (pure virtual interface + structs), Foo.h (class declaration with mutex_, logger_, internal maps), Foo.cpp (implementation with lock_guard, generateId, timestamps). Tests use doctest TEST_CASE/SUBCASE with direct instantiation. CMakeLists.txt adds static lib linked to threatone_compiler_options and threatone_core. Tests link to module libs via tests/CMakeLists.txt.
directory_structure: src/<module>/include/<module>/ for headers, src/<module>/src/ for .cpp files, tests/<module>/ for test files. cmake/ for build helpers. third_party/ for vendored deps.
